type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The ammonia and urea plants were shut down for approximately 3 months in 2014 due to an unplanned outage resulting in reduced VOC emissions.  In 2015, they operated 97%  of the time and 90% of the time respectively, which is more typical of plant operations resulting in higher annual emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Carbon monoxide is released as a byproduct of combustion and as a vented emission of process gas during startup and shutdown of the ammonia plants. As the number and duration of plant startup and shutdown events will vary year to year depending on plant reliability and turnaround schedules, the quantity of carbon monoxide emitted from venting of process gas can vary significantly from year to year. In 2013, there was a scheduled turnaround between August and October resulting in venting of process gas containing carbon monoxide. The startup of the plant in October required extensive venting of process gas containing carbon monoxide due to catalyst reduction venting. In addition, there were unplanned outages in November and December resulting in additional venting of process gas containing carbon monoxide. In 2013, a total of 1290 tonnes of carbon monoxide was vented during seven planned and unplanned ammonia plant outages. In 2014, there were five outages with no catalyst reductions that resulted in only 305 tonnes of carbon monoxide venting.

comments.comment:
Emissions to air are estimated using natural gas combustion emission factors and can vary year to year depending on operating hours and rates (AP42 Chapter 1.4).  Ammonia plant onstream time in 2019 was 81% compared with 94% in 2018.

In addition industrial wastewater is permitted to be used for crop irrigation and was previously reported as "Release to Land - Other" (2018 and prior), now reported in Disposal section (2019).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Carbon monoxide is released as a byproduct of combustion and as a vented emission of process gas during startup and shutdown of the ammonia plants. As the number and duration of plant startup and shutdown events will vary year to year depending on plant reliability and turnaround schedules, the quantity of carbon monoxide emitted from venting of process gas can vary significantly from year to year. In 2015, there were only two brief outages of the ammonia plant that vented a total of 319 tonnes of carbon monoxide. This is in addition to annual combustion emissions of carbon monoxide. In 2016, there was a maintenance outage that resulted in extensive venting of process gas containing 8% carbon monoxide. This venting event emitted approximately 914 tonnes of carbon monoxide over a three day period.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increased emissions due to increased fuel consumption - Ammonia plant onstream time in 2020 was 98% compared with 81% in 2019 resulting in increased combustion emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emissions increased compared to the previous year as the NH3 plant only operated 81% of the year in 2019 compared to 98% in 2020.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Carbon monoxide emissions can vary significantly year to year depending on the number of planned and unplanned ammonia plant outages as carbon monoxide in process gas is vented during plant start up and shut down events. In 2020 there was only 1 ammonia plant outage compared to 3 outages in the previous year.
The decrease in CO emission to reduced shutdown venting events was somewhat offset by an increase in combustion emissions: emissions to air that are estimated using natural gas combustion emission factors can vary year to year depending on operating hours and rates (AP42 Chapter 1.4). Ammonia plant onstream time in 2020 was 98% compared with 81% in 2019 resulting in increased combustion emissions.
In addition, CO results for the Urea main stack have been historically calculated using combustion emission factors for natural gas consumed as results are normally below method detection limit during source testing.  In 2020, CO was detected during source testing and this result was used in place of the combustion emission factors.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Methanol is produced as a byproduct in the ammonia plant natural gas reforming process.  Emissions in 2020 increased compared to 2019 as the ammonia plant operated 98% of the time in 2020 vs 81% of the time in 2019.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emissions to air are estimated using natural gas combustion emission factors and can vary year to year depending on operating hours and rates (AP42 Chapter 1.4). Ammonia plant onstream time in 2020 was 98% compared with 81% in 2019 resulting in increased combustion emissions.

comments.comment: There can be significant annual variation in source testing results.  In 2021 there was a large increase in particulate from the Urea Main Stack.

comments.comment: Installation of a medium pressure condensate stripper in 2022 reduced NOx emissions from the primary reformer stack.  The full impact of this change is evident in 2023.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: A medium pressure condensate stripper was installed in 2022. Part of the impact of this equipment is the ability to recycle the methanol produced in the primary reformer back into the process.  This eliminated methanol emissions from the primary reformer stack. The full impact of this change is evident in 2023.
